region_shape: A 2-block shared continuation: region HEAD (r-=0x22 + an internal if(token&2) branch) -> region TAIL (r^=0x33), reached from two paths A and B. A single ENTRY handler routes to A or B with clean immediate state writes (no reg-resident selector), so L7 isolates the multi-block region and does NOT drag in L2. The internal branch makes the region irreducibly 2 blocks (a straight sequence would merge in the render).

glbopt1_structure.shared: region head (K2) is written by BOTH A(11) and B(12) -> 2 preds = SHARED region

phase1_join_preserve.decision: RECOMMENDED / correct deobfuscation -- a join, not duplication (no bloat).
phase1_join_preserve.mechanism: the general unflatten rule (region-aware const set incl KENTRY+RT): redirect every genuine dispatcher-returning state-writer to its routed handler; TERM-writers -> terminal. The region head naturally becomes a JOIN (its 2 writers A,B both route to it); its internal branch is preserved because only its ARMS (not the head) are redirected.
phase1_join_preserve.render: if(token&1) r+=0x11 else r^=0x55; r-=0x22 (shared join, ONCE); if(token&2) r^=0x33; return. The region head `- 0x22` appears EXACTLY ONCE (assert count==1) -- a true shared join.

phase2_deshare_duplicate.decision: per the L7 spec -- duplicate the 2-block region into each path.
phase2_deshare_duplicate.mechanism: TWO PASSES via optblock re-entry. Pass 0: drain the entry (KENTRY/K0/K1 writers -> their handlers) AND clone the region HEAD (conditional) per path A,B via queue_create_conditional_redirect(ref=head, conditional_target=tail, fallthrough=term) -- jcc SENSE resolved by checking which head arm writes RT (taken_to_tail); here taken arm 15 writes TERM not RT, so cond_t=term / fallthrough=tail (swapped). Pass 1: the now-multi-pred TAIL is de-shared P3-style (per-pred state-free create_and_redirect copy). Tail re-identified across the serial-shifting pass-0 by instruction-EA overlap.
phase2_deshare_duplicate.render: if(token&1){ r+=0x11; r-=0x22; if(token&2) r^=0x33; } else { r^=0x55; r-=0x22; if(token&2) r^=0x33; } return. The region (head+internal-branch+tail) is DUPLICATED into both arms. The head `-0x22` folds in the token&1 arm (token+0x18-0x22 = token-0xA), so witness the un-folding internal `token & 2` branch + `^0x33` tail appearing TWICE.

key_findings: Multi-block region with an internal branch CAN'T be a single-block P3 de-share or a single create_and_redirect -- the internal jcc must be cloned (create_conditional_redirect) and the tail de-shared separately.

De-share of a 2-block region = clone the conditional HEAD per path (create_conditional_redirect) THEN de-share the now-multi-pred TAIL P3-style. Two optblock passes avoid cross-insert serial prediction; re-identify the tail by instruction-EA overlap after the pass-0 serial shift.

create_conditional_redirect jcc SENSE: wire conditional_target to whichever outcome the ref block's jz-taken (tail.d.b) leads to; detect by which head arm writes the tail state (RT). Here the taken arm wrote TERM, so cond/fallthrough were swapped.
Join-preservation (phase 1) is the correct bloat-free reconstruction; duplication (phase 2) is the niche capability. Both proven.
test_summary: tests/system/runtime/hexrays/test_insert_unflatten_mini.py::TestInsertUnflattenRegion (dump + join + de-share); 17 passed / 1 xfailed across the lab file.
